items.title: Microsoft says it will not pursue security researchers after zero-day backlash
items.summary: Microsoft said it is taking the feedback seriously, adding: “To be clear about our approach to legal matters, we have no intention to pursue action against individuals conducting or publishing their security research.”

items.title: Webinar tomorrow: From alert to resolution in network incident response
items.summary: Network incidents are often detected quickly, but investigations and coordination can delay resolution. Join our webinar tomorrow to learn how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help IT teams accelerate incident response. [...]

items.title: As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution
items.summary:
AI’s use in the military is part of the administration’s larger push to grow the capability it sees as a unique American advantage.
The post As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft confirms outage affecting MFA, My Sign-Ins platform
items.summary: Microsoft is working to address an ongoing incident preventing customers from setting up multi-factor authentication (MFA) or accessing the My Sign-Ins platform. [...]

items.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
items.summary:
Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory, reporting, maybe a compliance module bolted on the side. The work has since outgrown the descriptor.

A Security Growth Platform is the more precise name for what MSPs and MSSPs need from the software

items.title: 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access
items.summary:
Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has been released for the CIFSwitch flaw, which allows low-privileged users to escalate to root on vulnerable Linux systems.
The post 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft fixes KB5089549 Windows security update install issues
items.summary: Microsoft has resolved a known issue causing installation failures and 0x800f0922 errors when deploying the May 2026 Windows 11 security update (KB5089549). [...]

items.title: Containers on fire: from container escapes to supply chain attacks
items.summary: We break down the primary attack vectors in containerized environments: exposed secrets, privilege misconfigurations, API compromise, and supply chain attacks.

items.title: Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks
items.summary:
Hackers began exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS, four days after public disclosure.
The post Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI.

The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over 29,000 weekly downloads. The package is still available for download from the repository.

What

items.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
items.summary:
Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites.

WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed customizable Google Maps and OpenStreetMap with markers, listings, and advanced location features on WordPress sites. It is

items.title: Welcome NVIDIA Cosmos 3: The First Open Omni-model for Physical AI Reasoning and Action
datasette 1.0a32
items.summary:
Release: datasette 1.0a32
        A minor bugfix release. Fixes a bug with INSERT ... RETURNING queries via the new /db/-/execute-write endpoint and a bunch of base_url issues which showed up when I was experimenting with Service Workers yesterday.
    
    
        Tags: datasette, annotated-release-notes

items.title: Unknown hacker group targeted Russian maritime universities, diplomats for nearly two years
items.summary: More than half of the attacks observed over the past year targeted educational institutions, particularly maritime universities and schools that train personnel for Russia's shipping, inland waterway and fishing industries.
